---
title: "CrisisCompanion: AI for Mental Health Crisis Support"
author:
  - name: Chris Endemann
    email: endemann@wisc.edu

date: 2025-09-11
date-format: long
image: "../../images/vincent-van-zalinge-mDohQISBnCk-unsplash.jpg"

categories:
  - Projects
  - ML Marathon
  - MLM25
  - NLP
  - LLM
  - GenAI
  - Mental health
  - Text data
  - UW-Madison

---

## About this project
CrisisCompanion was featured in the [2025 Machine Learning Marathon (MLM25)](https://ml-marathon.wisc.edu/), proposed by [Ross Jacobucci](https://centerhealthyminds.org/about/people/ross-jacobucci) from the [Center for Healthy Minds](https://centerhealthyminds.org/) at UW-Madison. This challenge explores how AI and NLP methods can support mental health crisis intervention -- an area where Ross's research on digital phenotyping and suicide risk detection intersects with applied ML.

### Links
- **Challenge proposer**: [Ross Jacobucci](https://rjacobucci.com/), Research Assistant Professor, Center for Healthy Minds, UW-Madison
- **ML Marathon**: [ml-marathon.wisc.edu](https://ml-marathon.wisc.edu/)

## Questions?
If you have any lingering questions about this project, please feel free to post to the [Nexus Q&A](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/ML-X-Nexus/discussions/categories/q-a) on GitHub. We will improve materials on this website as additional questions come in.
